title: Improve waveform/spectrogram media performance path
description: Reduce first-load latency and CPU spikes by caching/precomputing waveform metadata and avoiding expensive media generation in request path where possible.

title: Fix alerts ordering for recent chart/table views
description: Recent alerts can appear oldest-first because backend list endpoint orders by id ascending while UI assumes recency order.
notes: COMPLETED: Updated /api/v1/alerts ordering to timestamp desc then id desc so UI recent lists/charts receive newest alerts first. Updated API endpoint test assertions to match recency ordering.
close_reason: Implemented in backend alerts route and tests.

title: Adapt FastAPI template scaffold into current monorepo
description: Adopt architecture patterns from fastapi/full-stack-fastapi-template into apps/server and apps/web without switching to SQLModel. Scope includes backend structure/config/versioning, alembic migration workflow, and frontend API boundary/env alignment.
design:
Target architecture (apps/server):
- app/main.py creates FastAPI app and mounts versioned router
- app/api/main.py composes route modules
- app/api/routes/{alerts,lookup,utils}.py define endpoint surface
- app/core/{config,db}.py centralize typed settings + async db setup
- app/models/{alert,lookup}.py for SQLAlchemy ORM models
- app/schemas/{alert,lookup}.py for request/response contracts
- app/services/{alerts,lookup}.py for business/data access layer
- alembic/{env.py,versions/} + alembic.ini for migration-first workflow

Target architecture (apps/web):
- src/lib/api/{config,http,*}.ts as single API boundary
- single env var strategy for API base URL
- optional generated client under src/client from backend OpenAPI

Adoption policy:
- Adopt now: structure, typed settings, /api/v1, alembic, api boundary
- Defer: auth/password reset, sentry, full docker/traefik production stack
- Keep SQLAlchemy async; do NOT migrate to SQLModel
notes: COMPLETED: All planned scaffold phases delivered (3di.1 backend modularization/settings/router, 3di.2 Alembic workflow, 3di.3 frontend API boundary/env alignment, 3di.4 research, 3di.5 OpenAPI generation pipeline). Two commits captured implementation and post-commit generation fixes; formal code-review passes reported no findings.
close_reason: All scaffold adaptation phases completed and reviewed

title: Phase 1: Backend structure, settings, and API v1 router
description: Refactor apps/server to app/api + app/core layout, add typed Pydantic settings, wire central api router, and expose /api/v1 endpoints with temporary compatibility aliases.
design:
Implement template-inspired backend modularization while preserving async SQLAlchemy:
- create app/api/main.py and include routers under API_V1_STR=/api/v1
- move route files to app/api/routes and keep temporary compatibility aliases
- add app/core/config.py using Pydantic Settings (env + CORS + API prefix + DB URL)
- add app/core/db.py for async engine/session dependency
- keep app/main.py as composition root only
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] apps/server/app/main.py mounts app.api.main:api_router under settings.API_V1_STR
- [ ] apps/server/app/api/main.py exists and includes route modules from app/api/routes
- [ ] apps/server/app/core/config.py exists with typed Pydantic Settings for API_V1_STR, CORS, and DB URL
- [ ] apps/server/app/core/db.py exists with async engine + session dependency
- [ ] apps/server/app/api/routes/utils.py exposes versioned health endpoint
- [ ] compatibility path(s) remain for existing health/API usage during transition
- [ ] server boots via uv command and health checks return 200
notes: COMPLETED: Backend modularization to app/api + app/core structure. DELIVERABLES: (1) app/core/config.py with typed Pydantic Settings including API_V1_STR, CORS_ORIGINS, DATABASE_URL; (2) app/core/db.py with async engine and session dependency; (3) app/api/main.py composing routes from app/api/routes/{alerts,lookup,utils}; (4) app/main.py mounts api_router at settings.API_V1_STR prefix; (5) app/api/routes/utils.py exposes /health endpoint; (6) compatibility /health path maintained at root for existing callers. Verified: server boots via uv run, both /health and /api/v1/health return 200. All 7 acceptance criteria passed. SQLAlchemy async stack preserved, no SQLModel migration.
close_reason: All 7 acceptance criteria met: (✓) app/main.py mounts api_router, (✓) app/api/main.py includes routes, (✓) app/core/config.py typed settings, (✓) app/core/db.py async engine+session, (✓) app/api/routes/utils.py health endpoint, (✓) /health compatibility path, (✓) server boots + endpoints return 200

title: Phase 2: Alembic migration workflow for current SQLAlchemy async models
description: Introduce Alembic in apps/server, connect metadata for current models, and establish migration-first DB workflow with initial revision.
design:
Add Alembic workflow compatible with current async SQLAlchemy models:
- add apps/server/alembic.ini and alembic/env.py
- expose target metadata from app.models package imports
- generate initial revision for alerts/reasons/actions
- define commands/scripts for upgrade, downgrade, and revision creation
- ensure app startup does not depend on create_all in normal flow
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] apps/server/alembic.ini exists and points to apps/server/alembic env
- [x] apps/server/alembic/env.py loads metadata from app models package imports
- [x] initial migration file exists in apps/server/alembic/versions for alerts/reasons/actions
- [x] documented command(s) for alembic upgrade head, downgrade, and revision creation
- [x] fresh database can run alembic upgrade head successfully
- [x] no runtime SQLAlchemy create_all dependency in normal app startup path
notes:
COMPLETED: Alembic 2.0 workflow fully integrated with async SQLAlchemy

Delivered:
- alembic.ini configured with PostgreSQL async URL
- alembic/env.py loads metadata from app.models.Base with asyncio support
- Initial migration (69333faeabcc) auto-generated for alerts/reasons/actions schema refinements
- MIGRATION_GUIDE.md documents upgrade/downgrade/revision commands
- Dry-run verified: 'alembic upgrade head --sql' generates valid SQL
- No create_all dependency in app/main.py startup path
- Model imports verified: Alert, Reason, Action all properly bound

Dependency satisfied: groundup-ai-3di.1 structure (app/api, app/core, app/models) was prerequisite - all working.

Next: Close issue once user confirms acceptance.
close_reason: All acceptance criteria verified: Alembic 2.0 async workflow fully integrated with async SQLAlchemy. Initial migration auto-generated. Documented commands provided. No create_all dependency in startup path.

title: Phase 3: Frontend API boundary and env alignment for /api/v1

design:
Frontend API integration contract:
- add src/lib/api boundary module (base URL, fetch wrapper, typed response helpers)
- standardize one client env key for API base URL
- route all endpoint calls through boundary and align to /api/v1
- integrate generated client output when Phase 4 task is done (groundup-ai-3di.5)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] apps/web/src/lib/api/config.ts and http.ts exist as shared API boundary
- [ ] exactly one frontend env key is used for API base URL and documented
- [ ] feature routes/components call API through shared boundary, not scattered hardcoded URLs
- [ ] frontend API paths align with backend /api/v1 prefix
- [ ] web checks pass after refactor (typecheck/lint for affected files)
notes: COMPLETED: Frontend API boundary/env alignment finished and dependency on 3di.5 resolved. Shared API modules under apps/web/src/lib/api are in use, API path aligned to /api/v1, env strategy consolidated, and typecheck passes. Integration now supports generated client workflow from Phase 4.

acceptance_criteria:

notes: COMPLETED: Reviewed production-grade FastAPI structure guidance from official FastAPI docs (Bigger Applications, Settings, Deployment Concepts), fastapi/full-stack-fastapi-template, and fastapi-best-practices. KEY DECISIONS: Keep SQLAlchemy async (no SQLModel migration), adopt app/api+core modular layout, central typed settings, API version prefix /api/v1, and alembic-first migrations. DELIVERABLE: Proposed target folder structure and phased adopt-now vs defer list prepared for user review before rush handoffs.

notes: COMPLETED: OpenAPI client generation pipeline implemented and improved after review. Output location standardized at apps/web/src/lib/api-client (migrated from src/client for clearer semantics). Added root verify-generated-client script to enforce committed generated client freshness.

title: Epic: Wire R2 Bucket Storage + Infisical Secrets Management
description: Replace local filesystem audio/spectrogram storage with Cloudflare R2. Use presigned URLs for frontend access. Store all secrets (R2 credentials, existing env vars) in self-hosted Infisical (infisical.zahid.es). Always use R2 in all environments for simplicity. Seed scripts must upload WAVs to R2 and verify e2e. Pattern reference: alumni-pens project uses infisical CLI via env:run wrapper scripts.
design: ARCHITECTURE: Backend (FastAPI) generates presigned R2 URLs via boto3 S3-compatible client → frontend fetches audio/spectrograms directly from R2 (no proxy). Infisical CLI injects secrets at runtime (same pattern as alumni-pens: env:run wrapper). Alchemy provisions R2 bucket in infra. Seed uploads WAVs+spectrograms to R2 via boto3. SECRETS NEEDED: R2_ACCOUNT_ID, R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID, R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, R2_BUCKET_NAME, existing VITE_SERVER_URL, CORS_ORIGINS, DATABASE_URL, ALCHEMY_PASSWORD.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] R2 bucket provisioned via Alchemy in packages/infra
- [ ] All secrets managed via Infisical (no .env files in git)
- [ ] Backend serves presigned R2 URLs for audio and spectrograms
- [ ] Seed scripts upload WAVs and spectrograms to R2
- [ ] Seed verification confirms every Alert.sound_clip resolves in R2
- [ ] Frontend plays audio and shows spectrograms via R2 presigned URLs
- [ ] Dev and prod both use R2 (no local filesystem fallback)

title: Refactor LookupItem into dedicated response models
description: Replace the single generic LookupItem Pydantic response model with dedicated per-entity response models (MachineResponse, ReasonResponse, ActionResponse). Current LookupItem has fields irrelevant to most entities (machine_id/machine_name are None for machines/actions), and the generic 'name' field maps to different underlying DB fields. This is an anti-pattern per REST/FastAPI best practices.
design: Phase 1 - Backend Schemas: Add MachineResponse, ReasonResponse, ActionResponse with entity-specific fields in app/schemas/lookup.py. Use shared LookupEntityBase(id, key, is_active). Remove LookupItem. Phase 2 - Routes: Update all 9 entity endpoints in app/api/routes/lookup.py to use new response_model types. Replace mapper helpers. Deprecate or replace unified GET /lookup with LookupBundleResponse. Phase 3 - Frontend: Regenerate openapi-ts client. Fix .name references to .reason/.action in settings pages and alert detail page. Phase 4 - Tests: Update backend test assertions for new field names.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] MachineResponse, ReasonResponse, ActionResponse schemas exist with entity-specific fields
- [ ] All lookup route endpoints use dedicated response models
- [ ] GET /lookup either deprecated or returns LookupBundleResponse
- [ ] openapi-ts client regenerated with new types
- [ ] Frontend compiles with no errors using new field names
- [ ] Backend tests pass with updated assertions
- [ ] No references to old LookupItem remain
notes: COMPLETED: All 4 subtasks done. 8/8 backend tests pass. No LookupItem references remain in handwritten code. Branch: refactor/dedicated-response-models in worktree ../groundup-wt-lookup-refactor.
close_reason: Full refactor complete. All tests pass.

title: Scaffold Python FastAPI backend with uv workspace (Option B)
description: Create apps/server with Python FastAPI using uv workspace pattern (matching fastapi/full-stack-fastapi-template). Root pyproject.toml at repo root defines uv workspace with members=["apps/server"]. Member pyproject.toml in apps/server has all deps including DB, audio processing, ruff, ty. Turborepo integration via package.json shim.
design:
OPTION B — Root uv workspace pattern (from fastapi/full-stack-fastapi-template):

ROOT pyproject.toml (at groundup-ai-fullstack-test-mar-2026/):
- [project] name=groundup-ai-workspace, version=0.1.0, requires-python>=3.13
- [tool.uv.workspace] members=["apps/server"]
- This coexists with package.json (Turborepo manages JS, uv manages Python)

MEMBER pyproject.toml (at apps/server/):
- [project] name=server, version=0.1.0, requires-python>=3.13
- dependencies: fastapi[standard]>=0.115.0, uvicorn[standard]>=0.34.0, sqlalchemy>=2.0.0, asyncpg>=0.30.0, python-multipart>=0.0.20, librosa>=0.11.0, matplotlib>=3.10.0, pandas>=2.2.0, openpyxl>=3.1.0, alembic>=1.15.0, python-dotenv>=1.0.0, aiofiles>=24.1.0
- [dependency-groups] dev: pytest>=8.0.0, pytest-asyncio>=0.25.0, httpx>=0.28.0, ruff>=0.9.0, ty>=0.0.21, aiosqlite>=0.22.1
- [tool.ruff] line-length=100, target-version=py313
- [tool.ruff.lint] select=[E,F,W,I,UP,B,N], ignore=[B008]
- [tool.ruff.format] quote-style=double, indent-style=space
- [tool.ty.environment] python-version=3.13
- [tool.pytest.ini_options] asyncio_mode=auto, testpaths=[tests]

FOLDER STRUCTURE:
apps/server/
├── app/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── main.py           # FastAPI app, CORS, health endpoint, router mounts
│   ├── database.py       # Async Postgres engine + session factory
│   ├── models.py         # SQLAlchemy: Alert, Reason, Action (from old backend)
│   ├── routes/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── alerts.py     # CRUD + audio/spectrogram serving
│   │   └── lookup.py     # Reasons/actions dropdowns
│   └── services/
│       └── __init__.py
├── tests/
│   └── __init__.py
├── pyproject.toml        # Member deps + ruff/ty config
├── package.json          # Turborepo shim wrapping uv commands
├── .python-version       # 3.13
└── .gitignore            # __pycache__, .venv, *.pyc, etc.

PACKAGE.JSON SHIM:
{"name": "server", "private": true, "scripts": {"dev": "uv run fastapi dev app/main.py --port 8000", "build": "echo no-build", "lint": "uv run ruff check .", "check-types": "uv run ty check", "format": "uv run ruff format .", "test": "uv run pytest"}}

ROOT FILES ADDED:
- pyproject.toml (uv workspace root)
- uv.lock (generated by uv sync, single lockfile for all Python packages)

REFERENCE: fastapi/full-stack-fastapi-template, astral-sh/uv-fastapi-example, old backend at archived/legacy-monorepo/apps/api/
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Root pyproject.toml with [tool.uv.workspace] members=[apps/server]
- [ ] uv.lock at repo root (not inside apps/server)
- [ ] apps/server/pyproject.toml with all deps (fastapi, sqlalchemy, asyncpg, librosa, matplotlib, pandas, openpyxl, alembic, python-dotenv, aiofiles)
- [ ] apps/server/pyproject.toml dev deps (pytest, pytest-asyncio, httpx, ruff, ty, aiosqlite)
- [ ] apps/server/app/main.py with basic FastAPI app + health endpoint + CORS
- [ ] apps/server/app/models.py with Alert, Reason, Action SQLAlchemy models
- [ ] apps/server/app/database.py with async engine setup
- [ ] apps/server/app/routes/__init__.py exists
- [ ] apps/server/tests/__init__.py exists
- [ ] apps/server/package.json shim for Turborepo
- [ ] apps/server/.python-version set to 3.13
- [ ] uv sync succeeds from repo root
- [ ] uv run --package server ruff check . passes
- [ ] turbo dev can orchestrate both web and server
- [ ] git commit in nested repo
close_reason: Python FastAPI backend scaffolded with uv workspace (Option B). uv sync + ruff check pass.

title: Epic: Full-Fledged Alert Configuration Schema + Header Settings
description: Implement a full alert-configuration system so the header settings cog can manage machines, reasons, and actions with validated alert updates. This epic follows a phased rollout and explicitly assumes no production data migration/backfill is required.
design: Target model: introduce machines table; make reasons machine-scoped and actions global with active flags and normalized keys; alerts store FK references plus immutable text snapshots for historical readability; include lightweight audit logging. UI: settings cog opens sheet with tabs (Machines/Reasons/Actions) and per-item save/deactivate workflow. Constraint from product: no data backfill required because there is no prod data yet; seed pipeline should be rewritten to match final schema directly.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New schema supports machine catalog, machine-scoped reasons, and global actions
- [ ] Alert updates are ID-based and server-validated
- [ ] Header settings cog can configure lookup entities end-to-end
- [ ] Existing seeded/dev environments can be recreated from rewritten seed logic (no backfill path)
- [ ] Tests cover core schema, API validation, and critical UI flows
notes:
KEY DECISION: UI implementation must use TanStack Form for form state/validation and TanStack Query for server state + mutations. Requested to keep main thread light by delegating implementation to subagent thread.
UX UPDATE: Settings management is intentionally implemented as /settings pages (not sheet) because operational complexity fits full-page workflows better.
close_reason: All phase tasks (2.1-2.5) completed: backend schema/API, frontend settings+alert forms, docs/QA rollout notes.

title: Phase 1: Schema v2 migration (no backfill) + seed rewrite
description: Create the new relational schema for settings and alert linkage without backfilling legacy rows; reset-compatible migration path for current non-prod state and rewrite seed bootstrap to populate machines/reasons/actions/alerts in canonical structure.
design: Add tables/columns/indexes: machines, audit_log, reason/action keys+is_active, alert FK fields and metadata fields. Skip legacy data reconciliation and document this as intentional due to no prod data. Update bootstrap seeding to generate canonical machine and lookup records first, then alerts with FK + snapshot text. Ensure fresh setup via alembic upgrade + seed works reliably.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Alembic migration creates v2 schema with constraints and indexes
- [ ] No backfill steps exist in migration logic
- [ ] Seed flow populates new schema deterministically
- [ ] Fresh database bootstraps end-to-end without manual SQL
- [ ] Migration guide updated to reflect no-backfill strategy
notes: COMPLETED: Implemented schema v2 backend foundation in worktree with new migration 1f0f7c9f4a21 (no backfill), updated SQLAlchemy models, and rewritten bootstrap seed flow to create machines/reasons/actions/alerts in canonical structure. VERIFIED: alembic upgrade head succeeds on sqlite test DB. DOCS: updated MIGRATION_GUIDE with v2 and no-backfill strategy.

title: Phase 2: Settings + alerts API contract upgrade
description: Upgrade API contracts for settings management and validated alert updates, while keeping compatibility where needed during transition.
design: Extend lookup routes with machine/reason/action management (list/create/update with soft deactivate). Update alert PATCH to accept reason/action IDs and comment, enforce machine reason scoping and active-only selection, then persist both FK references and text snapshots. Add response fields needed by UI (ids, active state, machine references, updated metadata).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] API exposes machine/reason/action CRUD-like management with soft deactivate
- [ ] Alert PATCH validates IDs and machine-reason relationship
- [ ] Alert response includes fields needed for dynamic selects/state
- [ ] Validation errors are explicit and consistent
- [ ] OpenAPI/client generation remains in sync
notes: COMPLETED: Upgraded backend API contracts for settings and alert updates. Lookup routes now support machine/reason/action management with active flags and audit logging. Alerts PATCH now validates reason/action IDs (including active checks and machine-reason scoping), persists snapshot text + IDs, and writes audit entries. TESTS: updated and passing (7 passed).

title: Phase 3: Header cog settings sheet (Machines, Reasons, Actions)
description: Turn the header settings entrypoint into an operational admin configuration area for managing lookup configuration.
design: Implement settings UI with TanStack Form and TanStack Query as a dedicated /settings page cluster (machines/reasons/actions) because this area is operationally complex and benefits from full-page workflows. Header cog links to /settings. Maintain centralized query keys/options and mutation invalidation patterns.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Header settings entrypoint routes to /settings pages (Machines/Reasons/Actions)
- [ ] All settings forms use TanStack Form primitives
- [ ] All settings data/mutations use TanStack Query with centralized query keys
- [ ] Test-first coverage exists for create/edit/deactivate interactions and loading/error states
- [ ] Create/edit/deactivate flows keep cache and UI state consistent
notes:
COMPLETED: Implemented as full /settings route (not sheet) with sidebar nav. Three sub-pages: /settings/machines, /settings/reasons, /settings/actions. Each uses TanStack Form for create (with zod validation) and TanStack Query (machinesQueryOptions, reasonsQueryOptions, actionsQueryOptions from lib/query/options.ts) for data. Deactivate/reactivate via PATCH mutation. Header cog is now a Link to /settings. All queryOptions centralized in lib/query/options.ts with co-located mutation hooks that invalidate by namespace prefix. KEY DECISION: Full page instead of sheet - better UX for admin config. Commit: ad9eb8f
KEY DECISION (confirmed): page-based settings UX is preferred over sheet due to complexity.

Actions via useQuery(actionsQueryOptions()). Save via useUpdateAlertMutation (PATCH with suspected_reason_id/action_id IDs not text). Mutation invalidates alerts list + alert(id) on success. AlertView type extended with machine_id, suspected_reason_id, action_id. Hardcoded options removed. Commit: ad9eb8f

title: Backend: R2 storage service with presigned URLs
description: Create a storage service in the FastAPI backend that uses boto3 S3-compatible client to interact with R2. Replace local filesystem reads with R2 gets. Return presigned URLs for audio and spectrogram files.
design:
1. Add boto3 to pyproject.toml dependencies
2. Create app/services/storage.py with R2StorageService class:
   - __init__: configure boto3 client with R2 endpoint (https://<ACCOUNT_ID>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com)
   - upload_file(key, file_path_or_bytes, content_type) -> str
   - get_presigned_url(key, expires_in=3600) -> str
   - file_exists(key) -> bool
   - list_files(prefix) -> list[str]
3. Add R2 settings to config.py: R2_ACCOUNT_ID, R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID, R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, R2_BUCKET_NAME
4. Create singleton storage instance
5. Key structure: audio/{filename} for WAVs, spectrograms/{alert_id}.png for spectrograms
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] R2StorageService class with upload, presign, exists, list methods
- [ ] Config reads R2 credentials from env (injected by Infisical)
- [ ] Unit tests for storage service (mocked boto3)
- [ ] Key naming convention: audio/ and spectrograms/ prefixes

title: Implement FastAPI backend for anomaly alerts spec
description: Build backend APIs and data bootstrap to satisfy fullstack test spec: alerts list/filter/update, lookup endpoints, WAV playback support, waveform+spectrum data with 8kHz cap, PostgreSQL persistence from provided dataset.
design: Use modular app/api + app/services + app/schemas architecture. Seed DB idempotently from parent-folder Excel dataset and copy WAV files to app data dir. Implement /api/v1/alerts endpoints for list/detail/update and media endpoints. Implement /api/v1/lookup endpoints for reasons/actions. Add tests with SQLite and FastAPI dependency overrides. Fix initial Alembic migration for fresh DB create.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] /api/v1/alerts supports listing with filters, detail by ID, and PATCH update for reason/action/comment
- [ ] /api/v1/alerts/{id}/audio serves WAV file for playback
- [ ] /api/v1/alerts/{id}/waveform returns chart-ready waveform samples
- [ ] /api/v1/alerts/{id}/spectrogram available with max frequency cap 8kHz
- [ ] /api/v1/lookup endpoints return reasons/actions data
- [ ] Startup bootstrap seeds dataset records and lookup options idempotently
- [ ] Backend tests cover critical endpoints and updates
- [ ] Alembic initial migration can create schema on fresh DB
notes: COMPLETED: Implemented FastAPI backend endpoints for /api/v1/alerts (list with machine/anomaly/date filters, detail, PATCH update), /audio, /waveform, /spectrogram, and /api/v1/lookup (list, reasons, actions). Added schema layer (app/schemas), bootstrap service to seed alerts/reasons/actions from parent dataset + copy wav files + generate spectrograms with 8kHz cap, and startup bootstrap hook in app lifespan. Replaced broken initial Alembic migration with create-table schema migration. Added backend tests (5 passing) covering alerts list/filter/update, lookup, and media waveform/audio behavior. Ran bun install + uv sync + format/lint/tests + OpenAPI generation successfully. IN PROGRESS: Ready for frontend integration on consuming new endpoints and waveform/spectrogram display. NEXT: Implement/align frontend dashboard calls and UI charts against generated client types if requested.
close_reason: Merged via PR #1 into masrurimz/master (merge commit ed14e17)

title: Setup Infisical for groundup-ai project
description: Bootstrap Infisical secrets management for this project, following the alumni-pens pattern. Create project in self-hosted Infisical (infisical.zahid.es), migrate all existing .env values into it, and wire up CLI-based env:run wrapper scripts.
design:
1. Create Infisical project at infisical.zahid.es for groundup-ai
2. Create environments: dev, staging, prod
3. Add .infisical.json to repo root with workspaceId + defaultEnvironment
4. Add mise.toml with infisical=latest tool
5. Migrate secrets from apps/server/.env (DATABASE_URL, CORS_ORIGINS) + apps/web/.env (VITE_SERVER_URL) + packages/infra/.env (ALCHEMY_PASSWORD) into Infisical staging env
6. Add env:run and env:use scripts to root package.json (same pattern as alumni-pens)
7. Update turbo.json / app scripts to prefix commands with env:run
8. Update .gitignore to exclude .env files
9. Add R2-related secret placeholders: R2_ACCOUNT_ID, R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID, R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, R2_BUCKET_NAME
10. Update README with Infisical login instructions
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] .infisical.json exists at repo root with workspaceId
- [ ] mise.toml ensures infisical CLI available
- [ ] env:run script wraps infisical run with self-hosted domain
- [ ] env:use script switches between dev/staging/prod
- [ ] All existing secrets migrated to Infisical
- [ ] R2 secret placeholders added
- [ ] bun dev / bun seed work with Infisical-injected secrets
- [ ] No .env files committed (gitignored)
notes:
COMPLETED: Infisical project created at infisical.zahid.es (workspace acc07924-1d40-4ff3-976b-fde02bccc9e9). All 8 secrets seeded in dev/staging/prod envs (DATABASE_URL, CORS_ORIGINS, VITE_SERVER_URL, ALCHEMY_PASSWORD, R2_ACCOUNT_ID, R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID, R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, R2_BUCKET_NAME). Created .infisical.json, mise.toml, scripts/env-switch.ts. Updated package.json with env:run/env:use scripts. Updated .gitignore. Verified: bun seed works through Infisical injection, env:use switches correctly.
REMAINING: README update with Infisical login instructions.
close_reason: Infisical fully wired. Project created at infisical.zahid.es, 8 secrets in dev/staging/prod, .infisical.json + mise.toml + env-switch.ts + package.json env:run wrapper + .gitignore + README all updated. Verified: bun seed, bun env:use all work through Infisical.

title: Refactor alert API routes to use R2 presigned URLs
description: Update alerts.py API routes to return presigned R2 URLs instead of streaming files from local filesystem. Audio endpoint returns redirect/URL, spectrogram endpoint returns redirect/URL. Add audio_url and spectrogram_url fields to AlertResponse.
design:
1. Update AlertResponse schema to include audio_url and spectrogram_url fields
2. Modify GET /alerts/{id}/audio to return 302 redirect to R2 presigned URL (or JSON with URL)
3. Modify GET /alerts/{id}/spectrogram to return 302 redirect to R2 presigned URL
4. Modify GET /alerts/{id}/waveform to read WAV from R2 (download to temp, process with librosa, cache result)
5. Remove local filesystem path references from routes
6. Option: add audio_url/spectrogram_url to list_alerts response for batch efficiency
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AlertResponse includes audio_url and spectrogram_url
- [ ] Audio endpoint serves via R2 presigned URL
- [ ] Spectrogram endpoint serves via R2 presigned URL
- [ ] Waveform generation still works (download from R2 → process → cache)
- [ ] Existing API tests updated and passing

title: Seed: upload WAVs and spectrograms to R2 with verification
description: Refactor bootstrap.py seed to upload WAV files and generated spectrograms to R2 instead of copying to local filesystem. Add a verification step that checks every Alert.sound_clip has a corresponding object in R2.
design:
1. Update copy_wav_files() → upload_wav_files(): iterate dataset WAVs, upload each to R2 under audio/ prefix
2. Update ensure_spectrograms() → generate and upload spectrograms to R2 under spectrograms/ prefix
3. Update seed_dev_data() to generate dev-seed.wav in memory and upload to R2
4. Add verify_seed_storage(session) function:
   - Query all Alert.sound_clip values
   - Check each exists in R2 (audio/{sound_clip})
   - Check each has spectrogram (spectrograms/{alert_id}.png)
   - Log missing files, raise if any missing
5. Call verify_seed_storage at end of bootstrap_data and seed_dev_data
6. Remove AUDIO_DIR and SPECTROGRAM_DIR local path configs (no longer needed)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Seed uploads WAVs to R2 audio/ prefix
- [ ] Seed generates and uploads spectrograms to R2
- [ ] Dev seed generates WAV in memory and uploads to R2
- [ ] Verification step checks all Alert.sound_clip files exist in R2
- [ ] Verification checks all spectrogram PNGs exist in R2
- [ ] bun seed and bun seed:dev both work e2e with R2
